title: Advisor System -- scheduled AI analysis agents with automated remediation
what: The advisor system is a registry of AI agents (advisors), each with a defined mandate, schedule, and preferred model. Advisors run on a schedule (nightly/weekly) and produce advisory reports plus remediation lugs. Ozi dispatches advisors. Each advisor reads spoke state, identifies gaps or risks, and writes findings as lugs.
why: Without advisors, spoke health checks require manual sessions. Advisors automate quality control - architecture review, spec fidelity, tool config drift, historical pattern analysis - without human-triggered sessions.

use_cases.title: Nightly spec fidelity check by Sage
use_cases.outcome: Sage scans WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl, identifies specs with no tests or stale content, creates remediation lugs

use_cases.title: Weekly architecture review by Archie
use_cases.outcome: Archie reviews impl lug patterns, flags architectural drift, writes advisory report

use_cases.title: Tool advisor audit on config drift
use_cases.outcome: Tool advisor compares tool configs against best practices, flags mismatches

patterns.name: Advisor is stub until activated
patterns.description: New advisors start with status=stub - registered but not dispatching. Activate by implementing the advisor script and setting status=active.

patterns.name: Advisor output = report + lugs
patterns.description: Every advisor run produces two outputs: a human-readable report and machine-actionable remediation lugs.

how.trigger: Ozi dispatch per run_schedule in schedule-index.json
steps_summary: 1. Ozi reads schedule-index.json, identifies advisors due to run
2. For each advisor: route to preferred_model_class
3. Advisor reads spoke state (lugs, skills, configs, code)
4. Advisor identifies gaps, risks, or improvements
constraints: Advisors detect and route - they never fix in place (detect-signal-route principle)
run_after field must be respected (Sage runs after Gardener)

not_when: Mid-implementation - advisors are analytical, not implementers

state_changes: last_run_at updated in registry.json and schedule-index.json

constraints: Advisors write to report + lugs only - never directly modify behavioral configs

open_questions: When does status change from stub to active?

body: The advisor system is a registry of AI agents, each with a defined mandate, schedule, preferred model class, and escalation path. Advisors run on a nightly or weekly schedule dispatched by Ozi (or manually via wai-tool-advisor skill). Each advisor reads the spoke state — lugs, sessions, skill files, configs — identifies gaps or risks within its mandate, then writes two outputs: a human-readable advisory report and machine-actionable remediation lugs in bytype/work/open/. Advisors are registered in WAI-Spoke/advisors/registry.json and scheduled in WAI-Spoke/advisors/schedule-index.json. The run_after field in schedule-index.json enforces sequencing (e.g., Sage runs after Gardener). New advisors start at status=stub and are promoted to status=active once their script is implemented. The detect-signal-route principle is mandatory: advisors detect problems and route findings; they never fix in place. Escalation paths are declared in each advisor's YAML charter — typically escalating to Ozi via a LOCAL-routed lug. Each advisor run appends a record to WAI-Spoke/advisors/{id}/runs.jsonl and emits a lifecycle event to WAI-Spoke/advisors/lifecycle.jsonl. Advisors that require pilot evaluation define a pilot_contract in their charter with KPIs, promotion criteria, and a minimum observation window before any behavioral conclusions are drawn. Advisor attribution fields (created_by_advisor, advisor_run_id, advisor_confidence) are required on all lugs authored by an advisor to enable ROI measurement.
done_when: Registry at WAI-Spoke/advisors/registry.json contains an entry for each active advisor with id, schedule, preferred_model_class, and status fields
Each advisor run appends a record to WAI-Spoke/advisors/{id}/runs.jsonl with trigger_type, findings_count, and completed_at
An advisor-generated lug includes created_by_advisor, advisor_run_id, and advisor_confidence fields
Advisors with run_after dependencies in schedule-index.json do not dispatch before their prerequisite completes in the same nightly run
An advisor at status=stub produces no dispatch output and does not appear in wakeup advisor summary
